HOW TO PLAY
  • Swipe below the Grid to move the white Orb.
  • Collect blinking crystals.
  • Avoid Block-A-Zoids.
  • Shake to Cosmic Zap.(OR DOUBLE TAP THE GRID)

This is honestly everything you need.

Curiosity, however, has poor impulse control. Keep scrolling for information you absolutely do not need to survive.

POINT SCORING

Crystal10
Color Complete100/ea
Time Alive1/sec
EXTRA
Gridlock Bonus (Relic, banked)300
Super Gridlock (banked)500/750/1000
Cosmic Zaps (unused)50/ea
Perfect Sector (no Zap)100

FULL-SURFACE CONTROL

Swipe anywhere on the visible game screen and the Orb moves by the same relative distance and direction, like a cursor. Touching the Orb never grabs or teleports it.

Start
Single-tap the open game surface from the start screen.
Move
Swipe anywhere with one finger. Lift and continue from any new touch point.
Cosmic Zap
Double-tap with one finger, or shake the phone.
Pause/Menu
Use the top-left control during play. The same menu remains available after Game Over.

THE PRISM GRID

ARKOM built the Prism Grid to hold fractured space together.

Fifteen sectors maintained its geometry, each carrying part of a vast stabilizing pattern. For a time, the Grid held.

Then the Block-A-Zoids invaded…

Self-replicating geometric machines falling into the Grid, breaking its alignments, and forcing entire sectors into Gridlock.

They don’t need to be fast. They just need you to be slow.

The Orb is the Grid’s final command core. Guided by you, it gathers scattered Prism Crystals, reconnects damaged pathways, and restores each sector before the swarm gets there first.

During Gridlock, lost Prism Relics may briefly emerge — fragments of the Grid’s original memory. Recovering them is optional. Surviving is not.

Cosmic Zaps are emergency discharges, powerful enough to erase every active Block-A-Zoid on the board. Scarce enough that you’ll want to save them for the moment you’re sure you need them. You won’t be sure.

Restore all fifteen sectors and the Grid begins again — under greater pressure. Each cycle brings the system closer to collapse.

Are you still reading this?!? …Go play the game!

HIGH SCORE 0
CYCLE— RELICS—
SECTOR— TIME—

The Prism Grid was built to keep perfect order.

Then protection became panic.

Its repair blocks turned on their own pattern, sealing red, green, and blue inside collapsing sectors.

Only one thing still moves free of the pattern: a rogue Orb.
